skill.name: meta-title-generator
skill.description: Generates evidence-based meta-analysis research titles from user keywords using PubMed literature search.
skill.summary: Creates bilingual (English/Chinese) meta-analysis titles by searching PubMed or using creative generation from keywords.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: After AI review, all 41 static analysis findings are false positives. The 15 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are triggered by non-code text in markdown and JSON files with zero cryptographic operations. The 17 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown formatting backticks for inline code and code blocks, not shell execution. The 2 'System reconnaissance' findings are iteration over API result data structures. The 7 network-related findings reference legitimate NCBI PubMed API calls with proper User-Agent headers. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscated code, or malicious intent was detected. This is a legitimate academic research tool. Residual low risk is documented for documented network access and script execution.

low_findings.title: Network access to NCBI PubMed API
low_findings.description: The skill uses urllib.request to call NCBI E-utilities API endpoints (esearch.fcgi and esummary.fcgi) for PubMed literature searches. This is legitimate academic use with proper User-Agent headers and no authentication tokens. Risk is limited to network access for a public API.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded external URLs
low_findings.description: The script contains hardcoded URLs to NCBI servers and the skill references a GitHub repository. All URLs point to legitimate public services (NCBI E-utilities API, GitHub) and do not indicate data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: External script execution reference
low_findings.description: SKILL.md references running python scripts/search_pubmed.py as part of the skill workflow. This is the skill's primary mechanism for PubMed integration. The executed script is part of the skill package and does not accept arbitrary user commands.

content.user_title: Generate Meta-Analysis Research Titles from Keywords
content.value_statement: Medical researchers struggle to create precise titles for systematic reviews and meta-analyses. This skill generates evidence-based or creatively derived meta-analysis titles from keywords by searching PubMed and returning bilingual output.
actual_capabilities: Searches PubMed in real time using user-provided keywords and returns relevant literature summaries
Generates 5 meta-analysis titles in both English and Chinese based on PubMed search results
Extracts PICOs (Population, Intervention, Comparison, Outcome, Study design) from found papers
Falls back to creative title generation when no papers are found on PubMed
Converts user keywords into optimized PubMed search strategies using AND/OR operators
limitations: Generates titles for meta-analysis and systematic review studies only, not for clinical trials
PubMed search is limited to 5 results maximum via the NCBI E-utilities API
Cannot access full-text papers or extract detailed statistical data from articles
Chinese translations may require verification by a native speaker for accuracy

use_cases.title: Medical Researcher Preparing a Systematic Review
use_cases.description: A researcher working on a new meta-analysis uses keywords to generate candidate titles based on existing PubMed literature and published PICOs.

use_cases.title: Graduate Student Drafting a Thesis Chapter
use_cases.description: A graduate student in epidemiology needs title ideas for their meta-analysis thesis chapter and uses keyword-based title generation.

use_cases.title: Clinical Reviewer Conducting Literature Synthesis
use_cases.description: A clinical reviewer searches PubMed and generates structured meta-analysis titles for a literature synthesis project.
use_cases.target_user: Clinical reviewers and evidence synthesis specialists

prompt_templates.title: Basic Keyword Title Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 5 meta-analysis titles using the keywords: [insert keywords]. Search PubMed for relevant literature first, then create titles based on the findings. Output each title in English and Chinese.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick title generation with minimal input

prompt_templates.title: PubMed Search Strategy Builder
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me build a PubMed search strategy for my meta-analysis on [topic]. My keywords are: [list keywords]. Convert these into a structured search string using AND/OR operators, then search PubMed and generate titles.

prompt_templates.title: PICO-Based Title Refinement
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the PubMed search results for [keywords], extract the PICO components from each paper found. Cross-match these components to create 5 original meta-analysis titles with strong research significance.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced title generation with evidence analysis

prompt_templates.title: Full Reproducible Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the meta-title-generator skill for [keywords]. Step 1: Build a PubMed search strategy. Step 2: Run scripts/search_pubmed.py with the strategy. Step 3: If results exist, analyze PICOs and generate 5 titles. Step 4: If no results, generate 5 creative titles. Step 5: Format output as bilingual JSON with English and Chinese for each title.

output_examples.output: The skill searches PubMed for lung cancer and immunotherapy literature, finds relevant papers, and generates 5 meta-analysis titles. Example titles include: 'PD-1 Inhibitors Combined with Chemotherapy for Advanced Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis' with its Chinese translation. All 5 titles are formatted in a structured JSON output.

The skill searches PubMed but finds no matching papers. It falls back to creative generation and produces 5 hypothetical meta-analysis titles based on the keywords, each with English and Chinese versions. A note explains that no PubMed literature was found and the titles are creatively derived.

best_practices: Use specific medical terms and drug names rather than general symptoms for better PubMed search results
Verify generated titles against existing published meta-analyses to avoid duplication
Review Chinese translations with a native speaker before submission to journals
anti_patterns: Using vague keywords like disease or treatment without specification leads to poor PubMed results
Requesting titles for clinical trials or primary research studies instead of meta-analyses
Expecting the skill to access full-text articles or extract statistical data from papers

faq.question: What types of titles can this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates titles specifically for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses, not for clinical trials or primary research studies.

faq.question: Does this skill search PubMed in real time?
faq.answer: Yes. It searches PubMed using the NCBI E-utilities API with your keywords and uses the results to inform title generation.

faq.question: What happens if no papers are found on PubMed?
faq.answer: The skill falls back to creative generation based on your keywords to produce plausible meta-analysis titles without literature support.

faq.question: Why are titles generated in both English and Chinese?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for bilingual output to support international research collaboration and Chinese-language academic publishing.

faq.question: Can I customize the number of titles generated?
faq.answer: The skill is designed to output exactly 5 titles. For different quantities, you would need to modify the output formatting instructions.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to search PubMed?
faq.answer: No. The NCBI E-utilities API used by this skill is publicly accessible without authentication for reasonable use.
